seoTitle: Marshall vs Marungi — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Marshall and Marungi. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Marshall and Marungi.
heading: Marshall vs Marungi — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Marshall, Victoria and Marungi, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marungi has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Marshall (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Marshall or Marungi safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Marshall has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Marungi scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Marshall scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Marungi scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Marshall or Marungi?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marungi has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
